title: First Chadian GSF Troops and Special Representative Arrive in Haiti
detail: The first contingent of Gang Suppression Force (GSF) troops from Chad arrived in Port-au-Prince on April 1, 2026, marking the operational beginning of the UN-backed GSF mission. Jack Christofides, a South African UN peacebuilding official appointed as GSF Special Representative, landed alongside the initial Chadian advance team of approximately 50 personnel. Unlike the predecessor Kenya-led Multinational Security Support (MSS) mission — which was limited to training and supporting the Haitian National Police — the GSF holds a broader mandate including independent counter-gang operations and arrest powers. Chad has pledged up to 800 total troops; approximately 300 more are expected by end of April, with full operational capacity of 5,550 personnel targeted by October 2026. The UNSC vote authorizing the GSF passed 12–0 with China, Russia, and Pakistan abstaining.

media.caption: First Chadian GSF troops arrive in Haiti alongside Special Representative Jack Christofides

title: Gran Grif and Kokorat San Ras Gangs Advance on Marchand Dessalines After Massacre
detail: Following the March 30, 2026 Jean-Denis massacre, Gran Grif forces and allied Kokorat San Ras gang repositioned to Pont Benoit and launched an offensive push toward Marchand Dessalines, approximately 19 km north in Haiti's Artibonite department. Videos on social media showed armed gang members distributing cash to residents in Marchand Dessalines — a tactic analysts interpreted as an attempt to establish civil compliance and control of the strategic agricultural region. Reuters journalists documented the continued attacks, confirming gangs were sustaining coordinated operations across multiple Artibonite communities in the days after the massacre. Gran Grif leader Luckson Elan justified the broader offensive as retaliation for a rival group's assault on his base at Savien. The advance deepened concerns about gang control of Haiti's most important rice-growing region and raised fears that the Artibonite breadbasket could be cut off ahead of planting season.
